name: SECOND BRAIN
_comment: Copy this file to config.json and fill it in. config.json stays on your machine — it is never served to the browser and is excluded from the shareable zip. Do NOT paste these keys into chat.

model._comment: The 'brain' that answers from your notes. EITHER native Claude (provider 'anthropic', model 'claude-opus-4-8', leave base_url empty) OR any OpenAI-compatible endpoint such as GLM 5.2 via OpenRouter/Z.ai (set base_url to the provider's /v1 URL and model to its slug). Get the exact base_url + slug from your provider's docs.

elevenlabs._comment: OPTIONAL premium voice. LEAVE THESE EMPTY and Jetty speaks in your browser's own built-in voice — free, no key, works out of the box. To upgrade to the cinematic 'Jetty' voice, paste your OWN ElevenLabs api_key (secret) + a voice_id. The voice_id is safe to share; the api_key is not.

agent._comment: Structural safety for /agent DRAFT mode — tools the agent may NOT use until you confirm an action. Wildcards match MCP tool names. These defaults let a draft read/research but never send, post, delete, spend, write, shell, or delegate. Override only if you know what you're doing.

telephony._comment: OPTIONAL — gives Jetty a real phone line so 'call …' / 'book a table' actually dials. Leave provider empty until you set up a Retell or Vapi account. VAPI: set assistant_id, phone_number_id, to. RETELL: set from, agent_id, to. Calling always asks you to confirm ('do it') first.

models._comment: OPTIONAL — extra named brains for the 'Jetty, try on <name>' hot-swap. Built-in spoken names already work: opus, sonnet, haiku, fable, and 'back to default'. Entries here inherit the top-level model block; entries with a base_url and an empty api_key automatically use the openrouter key below. Swaps are runtime-only and reset on restart.

openrouter._comment: OPTIONAL but the magic trick — ONE key, EVERY model. With this set, 'Jetty, try on grok / gpt / gemini / deepseek / llama / kimi…' fuzzy-matches the LIVE OpenRouter catalogue (newest match wins) and swaps brains on the spot. Get a key at openrouter.ai/keys.

openai._comment: OPTIONAL — GPT Realtime as a second live-voice engine for the 📡 button (true speech-to-speech, sub-second). GPT is only ears+mouth: the ask_jetty tool routes every knowledge question back to this server, so Claude + your vault stay the brain. Voice-pick the engine: 'use the realtime engine' / 'use the elevenlabs engine' / 'auto'.

hardware._comment: OPTIONAL — for 'Jetty, connect my AirPods' (needs `brew install blueutil` + your AirPods' Bluetooth address from System Settings → Bluetooth → ⓘ).

hue._comment: OPTIONAL — the room becomes Jetty's body: lights pulse emerald while he speaks, flash red on errors, dim in focus mode. Leave empty and it's a silent no-op. To pair: run the server, then say 'set up the lights' (press the round button on the Hue bridge first) — it auto-fills bridge_ip/app_key into jetty-hue.json. Or set them here by hand.

tools._comment: The 🔌 Tool Armory's engine. Tool runs execute on a fast Claude tier by default so answers stay quick even when your chat brain is a bigger model. You rarely need to change this.

telegram._comment: OPTIONAL 📱 Pocket Jetty: text your brain from your phone. 2 minutes: message @BotFather on Telegram, send /newbot, paste the token here, restart, then say 'Jetty, pocket code' and send that code to your bot. Reminders and urgent cards get pushed to your phone when you are away from the desk.

google._comment: OPTIONAL for the native Gmail and Google Calendar tiles. Easiest path: skip this and use Zapier for Gmail and Calendar instead (see GET-STARTED). Advanced: create an OAuth client at console.cloud.google.com and paste it here.

_duplex_comment: LIVE full-duplex voice (the 📡 button) uses ElevenLabs Agents with THIS server as the custom LLM, so the brain stays Claude + your vault. It needs (1) an ElevenLabs api_key WITH Agents/Conversational-AI scope (above), and (2) a public https tunnel to port 4722. See integrations/TAILSCALE.md, then run: python3 setup_duplex.py --url <public-url>. State lands in jetty-duplex.json (gitignored).
